summary: This week in AI news, OpenAI and Meta are making significant moves, with OpenAI expanding its ChatGPT capabilities and Meta facing backlash over its AI features. Meanwhile, Apple is suing OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft, and the EU is warning Meta about its autoplay and infinite scroll policies.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries is leading to a rise in concerns over data privacy, security, and ethics, highlighting the need for more transparency and regulation in the development and deployment of AI technologies.
highlights: OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, expanding its ChatGPT capabilities.
Meta is facing backlash over its AI features, including a controversial feature that allowed users to generate AI images based on public Instagram accounts.
Apple is suing OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft, accusing the company of stealing Apple secrets to advance its hardware plans.

articles.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
articles.description: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

articles.description: How Deutsche Telekom is becoming an AI-native telco with OpenAI-transforming customer service, employee workflows, network operations, and the future of voice.

articles.description: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.

articles.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
articles.description: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

articles.description: AI cheating leads to "a failed society," professor says.
